title: One-binary S4: deterministic pin-at-install + install.sh --binary mode installing a pre-approved artifact (checksum-verified, no local build)
summary: OPERATOR-BOOSTED to second-block priority 2026-07-13. APPROVED PLAN slice 4/5 (dep S2). install.sh: (1) after the build at install.sh:251, invoke "$INSTALL_LIB/evolve" reset-sha -operator so expected_ship_sha/expected_ship_version are pinned deterministically at install (today pinning is lazy first-run, verify.go:107-108); (2) add --binary <path-or-url> mode that SKIPS the local go build entirely and installs a pre-approved downloaded artifact, verifying its SHA256 against the release checksums.txt before placement — the corporate path: no compiler, no new fingerprint, machine-independent hash. Because the pin is version-aware (verify.go:117 re-pins silently across plugin version bumps), adopting each newly approved release needs zero manual pin management. Keep bash 3.2 compatible (repo shell conventions: set -uo pipefail, no mapfile/declare -A).
acceptance: install.sh --binary <artifact> installs without invoking go build; refuses on checksum mismatch (RED path tested in install e2e)
post-install .evolve/state.json carries expected_ship_sha + expected_ship_version matching the installed artifact
evolve loop --dry-run passes preflight on a --binary install with no compiler present
